# UC-11.3.61 · Proofpoint TAP Malicious URL Messages with Incomplete Link Rewriting

## Description

When Proofpoint reports malicious URL-laden messages where `completelyRewritten` is false, at least one dangerous link was not rewritten—users may still reach live phishing or exploit pages.

## Value

Prioritizes incidents for messaging engineering and SOC where URL Defense did not fully neutralize content, reducing reliance on mailbox rules alone.

## Implementation

Requires TAP SIEM message (blocked or delivered) events with `completelyRewritten` populated. Validate `threatType` extraction—some builds require `mvexpand` on `threatsInfoMap`. Pair with click telemetry (`proofpoint:click`) for the same GUID when available. Alert on any non-`na` rewrite failure in strict tenants.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint_tap_siem" earliest=-24h
| eval rewrite=coalesce(completelyRewritten, CompletelyRewritten, "")
| eval ttype=coalesce(threatType, ThreatType, "")
| where rewrite="false" AND (ttype="URL" OR match(lower(_raw), "(?i)url"))
| stats count values(subject) as subjects by sender, recipient, rewrite
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (sender, recipient, subject samples), single value (events per hour), timeline.
